description: The epic terminal -- we return to where this started. Re-run the quicksort.log trace (fz2 with telemetry) and confirm the re-lexing is gone: lexer.pass == number of sources, and the ~26ms visible re-lex plus the hidden per-call parse/env-rebuild are eliminated. Quantify what remains; close the original perf question as solved-by-construction, or re-scope any residual. Do not lose the other finding from the original investigation: file the ~47ms uninstrumented native backend tail (the codegen telemetry gap, post-drive in quicksort.log) as its own ticket. Outcome: back where we started, with updated docs and a tractable (or absent) problem.

comments.text:
Measurement from target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry /tmp/fz2_quicksort_12_9.jsonl run fixtures/quicksort/input.fz on 2026-06-10:

- Result: command exited 0 and printed the sorted quicksort output.
- Lexer span-starts: exactly 4, one each for fixtures/quicksort/input.fz, runtime:runtime.fz, runtime:Kernel.fz, runtime:Process.fz. No fragment pseudo-sources and no duplicate source lexing.
- Lexer elapsed total from span stops: 0.352 ms. The old visible ~26 ms source-fragment re-lex is gone.
- Legacy frontend/planner/type_infer telemetry: absent on fz2 path. The hidden per-call ModuleTypeEnv/reparse path is gone by construction after compiler2 contract/type resolution moved to TypeDefined facts.
- Remaining tail: fz.compiler2.drive 57.955 ms, then post-drive fz.codegen.compile 47.749 ms before fz.runtime.process_exited. Filed as fz-rh2.12.13.

title: Back here: re-examine quicksort.log; confirm/quantify the re-lex is gone
